name: Seymour Johnson AFB
tagline: Home of the F-15E Strike Eagle and KC-46 tankers in eastern North Carolina.
_note: Air Force fighter + tanker base (Goldsboro, eastern NC). CONUS full template (BAH MHA NC183 Seymour Johnson AFB; RentCast/SchoolDigger work). 4th Fighter Wing (F-15E Strike Eagle) + 916th Air Refueling Wing (KC-46, AF Reserve). Medical: 4th Medical Group clinic. Facts verified from official sources (seymourjohnson.af.mil, 4 MDG/TRICARE, DeCA/AAFES, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). North Carolina is TRICARE East. Affordable. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 27534 (Goldsboro).

data.Aircraft: F-15E Strike Eagle (plus KC-46 tankers, 916th ARW)
data.Nearby: Raleigh is about an hour-plus west

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Seymour Johnson Air Force Base is home to the 4th Fighter Wing, flying the F-15E Strike Eagle, and the 916th Air Refueling Wing with KC-46 tankers. It's a busy combat-aviation base.
It's in Goldsboro in eastern North Carolina, an affordable small city, with the Raleigh metro about an hour-plus to the west for big-city needs.

data.subtitle: Goldsboro is affordable and family-friendly. The main choice is in-town versus nearby communities, with Raleigh reachable for more.
intro: Eastern NC is inexpensive, so BAH stretches well. On-base housing is available; many also live off base in and around Goldsboro.
Where you live mostly comes down to commute and schools across Goldsboro and Wayne County, with the Raleigh area an option for those who don't mind the drive.

recommendations.advice: Goldsboro surrounds the base with affordable housing and the shortest commute.
On-base housing is available and convenient; weigh it against the affordable local market.
The Raleigh metro is about an hour-plus west for big-city shopping, dining, and an airport.
The area is affordable, so BAH tends to go far and many families buy.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD ID need a visitor pass and a REAL ID-compliant ID from the Visitor Control Center.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official Seymour Johnson AFB site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: Seymour Johnson AFB has privatized on-base family housing across several neighborhoods.
Eligibility and availability vary by rank and family size, so start with the housing office for the current manager, options, and waitlists.

source.label: DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA NC183 (Seymour Johnson AFB)

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools. Wayne County Public Schools serves Goldsboro and the area around the base.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Care: Outpatient primary and some specialty care on base; inpatient, emergency, and complex care via area network hospitals (and Raleigh)

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and Goldsboro's affordable market makes buying a real option for many families.
rent: Your tour is short (about two years or less) or your orders feel uncertain.
You want time to learn the area before committing.
You'd rather not gamble on resale timing when you PCS out.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in the areas you'd consider.
buy: You expect to be here three or more years.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
The affordable eastern NC market fits your budget for a home you like.
You've found a neighborhood and commute you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer, but Goldsboro's affordability makes buying worth a look for longer tours. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, weigh your tour length, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book on-base lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Start with the Housing Office
steps.text: Check on-base availability and get the office's read on Goldsboro before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and North Carolina vehicle registration, and learn the visitor-pass process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact Wayne County Public Schools early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment so you can be seen at the 4th Medical Group clinic or network providers.

data.q: What is Seymour Johnson known for?
data.a: It's home to the 4th Fighter Wing flying the F-15E Strike Eagle, plus the 916th Air Refueling Wing with KC-46 tankers.
On-base housing is available; many live off base in and around Goldsboro. The Raleigh metro is an option about an hour-plus west.
Yes. Eastern North Carolina is inexpensive, so BAH stretches well.
Seymour Johnson is MHA NC183. Use the calculator above for your grade.
